pull_request.title: feat: language-agnostic Helm chart and arbitrary namespace support
pull_request.body:
## Summary
- **Language-agnostic agent chart (#580)**: Remove Python bias from `mcp-mesh-agent` Helm chart. Add `agent.runtime` field and `isPython` helper for conditional Python env var injection. Remove dead `agent.script` and `agent.python` fields. Rewrite README with Python/TypeScript/Java examples.
- **Arbitrary namespace support (#579)**: Replace hardcoded FQDN hostnames with short names in `mcp-mesh-core` values. Add `| default .Release.Namespace` fallback to namespace template. Add `networkPolicy.allowedNamespace` for dynamic ingress rules. Document custom namespace, multi-tenant, and cross-namespace patterns.
- **ENTRYPOINT/CMD alignment**: Fix TypeScript runtime ENTRYPOINT (`node` → `npx tsx`). Align all scaffold Dockerfiles so CMD only provides the script/jar path (ENTRYPOINT provides the runtime command). Update Go handlers to match.
- **NetworkPolicy fix**: AND namespaceSelector with podSelector in registry ingress (was incorrectly OR'd as separate `from` entries).
- **Scaffold improvements**: Add `agent.runtime` to all helm-values templates. Add Docker/K8s/Documentation sections to Java/Python basic READMEs. Remove obsolete "Adding Tools" sections. Add cross-namespace comments to registryUrl.

## Review Notes
- Short service names replace FQDNs in mcp-mesh-core defaults — intentional for namespace flexibility, but a breaking change for cross-namespace users (documented override path)
- Duplicate PYTHONUNBUFFERED in ConfigMap + deployment env (minor, explicit env wins)

Closes #579
Closes #580

## Test plan
- [x] `helm lint` passes for all 3 charts (agent, core, registry)
- [x] `helm template` Python agent includes PYTHONUNBUFFERED/PYTHONPATH/UVICORN_* env vars
- [x] `helm template` TypeScript/Java agent does NOT include Python env vars
- [x] `helm template` with `--set agent.runtime=python` injects Python env vars regardless of image name
- [x] Registry NetworkPolicy renders AND'd selectors (namespace + podSelector in same from entry)
- [x] `go build` and `go test` pass for handlers
- [ ] Deploy Python/TypeScript/Java agents to K8s cluster and verify registration

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Multi-runtime agent support with runtime detection (Python, TypeScript, Java).
  * New agent.runtime configuration field.

* **Improvements**
  * Container images and starter commands updated to rely on base image entrypoints.
  * Network policies made namespace-aware with explicit ingress ports and allowedNamespace.
  * Service hostnames default to in‑namespace short names.

* **Documentation**
  * Expanded docs and Helm notes for custom namespaces, cross‑namespace access, and multi‑runtime deployments.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

-FROM mcpmesh/java-runtime:0.8
+FROM mcpmesh/java-runtime:0.9
 
 WORKDIR /app
 
@@ -23,4 +23,5 @@ USER mcp-mesh
 EXPOSE {{ .Port }}
 
 # Run the agent
-CMD ["java", "-jar", "target/{{ .NameKebab }}-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar"]
+# NOTE: Base image has ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar"], so CMD only needs the jar path
+CMD ["target/{{ .NameKebab }}-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar"]

@@ -39,6 +39,33 @@ The agent will start on port {{ .Port }} by default.
     └── {{ .NamePascal }}Application.java
 ```
 
+## Docker
+
+```bash
+# Build the image
+docker build -t {{ .Name }}:latest .
+
+# Run the container
+docker run -p {{ .Port }}:{{ .Port }} {{ .Name }}:latest
+```
+
+## Kubernetes
+
+```bash
+# Deploy using Helm
+helm install {{ .Name }} oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  -n mcp-mesh \
+  -f helm-values.yaml \
+  --set image.repository=your-registry/{{ .Name }} \
+  --set image.tag=v1.0.0
+```
+
+## Documentation
+
+- [MCP Mesh Documentation](https://github.com/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh)
+- [Java SDK Reference](https://github.com/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/tree/main/src/runtime/java)
+- Run `meshctl man decorators --java` for decorator reference
+
 ## License
 
 MIT

 ```
 
-## Adding New Tools
-
-To add a new tool, use the dual decorator pattern:
-
-```python
-@app.tool()
-@mesh.tool(
-    capability="my_capability",
-    description="Description of what this tool does",
-)
-async def my_tool(param: str) -> str:
-    """Tool docstring."""
-    return f"Result: {param}"
+## Docker
+
+```bash
+# Build the image
+docker build -t {{ .Name }}:latest .
+
+# Run the container
+docker run -p {{ .Port }}:{{ .Port }} {{ .Name }}:latest
 ```
 
-## Adding Dependencies
-
-To inject dependencies from other agents:
-
-```python
-@app.tool()
-@mesh.tool(
-    capability="my_capability",
-    dependencies=["other_capability"],
-    description="Tool with dependency injection",
-)
-async def my_tool_with_deps(
-    param: str,
-    other_capability: mesh.McpMeshTool = None,
-) -> str:
-    """Tool with injected dependency."""
-    if other_capability:
-        result = await other_capability()
-        return f"Got: {result}"
-    return "Dependency not available"
+## Kubernetes
+
+```bash
+# Deploy using Helm
+helm install {{ .Name }} oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  -n mcp-mesh \
+  -f helm-values.yaml \
+  --set image.repository=your-registry/{{ .Name }} \
+  --set image.tag=v1.0.0
 ```
 
+## Documentation
+
+- [MCP Mesh Documentation](https://github.com/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh)
+- [Python SDK Reference](https://github.com/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/tree/main/src/runtime/python)
+- Run `meshctl man decorators` for decorator reference
+

 MIT

@@ -153,6 +153,50 @@ helm install mcp-registry oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-registry \
   --namespace mcp-mesh
 ```
 
+### Custom Namespace
+
+By default, examples use `mcp-mesh` as the namespace. You can deploy into any namespace —
+just match `-n` with `--set global.namespace`:
+
+```bash
+# Deploy core to a custom namespace
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n my-namespace --create-namespace \
+  --set global.namespace=my-namespace
+
+# Deploy agent to the same namespace
+helm install my-agent oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n my-namespace \
+  -f helm-values.yaml
+```
+
+Service hostnames use short names (e.g., `mcp-core-mcp-mesh-registry`) that resolve
+within the same namespace automatically. No FQDN changes needed.
+
+For **multi-tenant** clusters (separate core per team), deploy each core to its own namespace:
+
+```bash
+# Team A
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n team-a --create-namespace --set global.namespace=team-a
+
+# Team B — same values, different namespace
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n team-b --create-namespace --set global.namespace=team-b
+```
+
+For **cross-namespace** access (agent in one namespace, core in another), use FQDNs:
+
+```bash
+helm install my-agent oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  --version 0.9.6 -n other-ns \
+  --set mesh.registryUrl=http://mcp-core-mcp-mesh-registry.team-a.svc.cluster.local:8000
+```
+
 ## Common Operations
 
 ```bash

@@ -1,220 +1,191 @@
 # MCP Mesh Agent Helm Chart
 
-This Helm chart deploys MCP Mesh agents with Python runtime on Kubernetes.
+This Helm chart deploys MCP Mesh agents on Kubernetes. It supports Python, TypeScript, and Java runtimes.
 
 ## Prerequisites
 
 - Kubernetes 1.19+
 - Helm 3.2.0+
 - MCP Mesh Registry deployed and accessible
-- Python-based MCP agent script
+- An MCP agent packaged as a Docker image
 
-## Installing the Chart
+## Quick Start
 
-### Method 1: Single-File Agent
-
-For simple agents with a single Python file:
+### Python Agent
 
 ```bash
-helm install hello-world ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
-  --set agent.name=hello-world \
-  --set agent.command='["/app/agent.py"]'
-```
-
-### Method 2: Multi-File Agent (Custom Docker Image)
-
-For complex agents packaged as Docker images with their own CMD:
-
-```bash
-helm install avatar ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
-  --set image.repository=myregistry/avatar-agent \
+helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
+  --set image.repository=myregistry/my-python-agent \
   --set image.tag=v1.0.0 \
-  --set agent.name=avatar \
-  --set agent.script=""
+  --set agent.name=my-agent
 ```
 
-The chart uses the Docker image's CMD instead of overriding it.
+Default image is `mcpmesh/python-runtime` — Python-specific env vars (`PYTHONUNBUFFERED`, `PYTHONPATH`, `UVICORN_*`) are auto-injected when the runtime is detected as Python (see [Runtime Detection](#runtime-detection)).
 
-### Method 3: External ConfigMap
-
-To install with an externally managed ConfigMap:
+### TypeScript Agent
 
 ```bash
-kubectl create configmap my-agent-code --from-file=agent.py=./my-agent.py
-helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent \
-  --set agentCode.enabled=true \
-  --set agentCode.configMapName=my-agent-code \
-  --set registry.url=http://mcp-mesh-registry:8080
+helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
+  --set image.repository=myregistry/my-ts-agent \
+  --set image.tag=v1.0.0 \
+  --set agent.name=my-agent
 ```
 
-### Method 4: Auto-Generated ConfigMap
-
-To install with automatic ConfigMap generation from a script file:
+### Java Agent
 
 ```bash
-helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent \
-  --set agentCode.enabled=true \
-  --set agentCode.scriptPath=scripts/my-agent.py \
-  --set registry.url=http://mcp-mesh-registry:8080
+helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
+  --set image.repository=myregistry/my-java-agent \
+  --set image.tag=v1.0.0 \
+  --set agent.name=my-agent
 ```
 
-Or using a values file:
+### Using a Values File
 
 ```bash
-helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -f examples/auto-configmap-values.yaml
+helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh -f my-values.yaml
 ```
 
 ## Uninstalling the Chart
 
-To uninstall the `my-agent` deployment:
-
 ```bash
-helm uninstall my-agent
+helm uninstall my-agent -n mcp-mesh
 ```
 
 ## Configuration
 
-The following table lists the configurable parameters of the MCP Mesh Agent chart and their default values.
-
 ### Key Parameters
 
-| Parameter       | Description                                         | Default                        |
-| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
-| `agent.name`    | Agent name for registry                             | `""`                           |
-| `agent.script`  | Python script path (single-file agents)             | `"/app/agent.py"`              |
-| `agent.command` | Container command override (empty = use Docker CMD) | `[]`                           |
-| `registry.host` | MCP Mesh Registry host                              | `"mcp-core-mcp-mesh-registry"` |
-| `registry.port` | MCP Mesh Registry port                              | `"8000"`                       |
+| Parameter          | Description                                         | Default                        |
+| ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ |
+| `image.repository` | Container image repository                          | `"mcpmesh/python-runtime"`     |
+| `image.tag`        | Image tag (overrides chart appVersion)              | `"0.9"`                        |
+| `agent.name`       | Agent name for registry                             | `""`                           |
+| `agent.command`    | Container command override (empty = use Docker CMD) | `[]`                           |
+| `registry.host`    | MCP Mesh Registry host                              | `"mcp-core-mcp-mesh-registry"` |
+| `registry.port`    | MCP Mesh Registry port                              | `"8000"`                       |
 
 ### Agent Configuration
 
-| Parameter                   | Description                                        | Default   |
-| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
-| `agent.name`                | Agent name override (empty = use from @mesh.agent) | `""`      |
-| `agent.version`             | Agent version                                      | `"1.0.0"` |
-| `agent.description`         | Agent description                                  | `""`      |
-| `agent.capabilities`        | List of capabilities provided                      | `[]`      |
-| `agent.dependencies`        | List of required dependencies                      | `[]`      |
-| `agent.healthCheck.enabled` | Enable health checks                               | `true`    |
+| Parameter                   | Description                                       | Default   |
+| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
+| `agent.name`                | Agent name override (empty = use from decorator)  | `""`      |
+| `agent.runtime`             | Runtime override (empty = auto-detect from image) | `""`      |
+| `agent.version`             | Agent version                                     | `"1.0.0"` |
+| `agent.description`         | Agent description                                 | `""`      |
+| `agent.capabilities`        | List of capabilities provided                     | `[]`      |
+| `agent.dependencies`        | List of required dependencies                     | `[]`      |
+| `agent.healthCheck.enabled` | Enable health checks                              | `true`    |
+| `agent.command`             | Container command override                        | `[]`      |
+| `agent.advertisedHost`      | Hostname advertised to registry                   | `""`      |
 
-### Agent Code Configuration
+### HTTP Configuration
 
-| Parameter                        | Description                                         | Default        |
-| -------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | -------------- |
-| `agentCode.enabled`              | Enable mounting agent code from ConfigMap           | `false`        |
-| `agentCode.configMapName`        | External ConfigMap name (for Method 2)              | `""`           |
-| `agentCode.scriptPath`           | Script path for auto-generated ConfigMap (Method 3) | `""`           |
-| `agentCode.mountPath`            | Mount path for agent code                           | `"/app/agent"` |
-| `agent.healthCheck.interval`     | Health check interval (seconds)                     | `30`           |
-| `agent.retry.attempts`           | Retry attempts                                      | `3`            |
-| `agent.retry.delay`              | Retry delay (seconds)                               | `5`            |
-| `agent.performance.timeout`      | Operation timeout (seconds)                         | `30`           |
-| `agent.performance.cacheEnabled` | Enable caching                                      | `true`         |
+| Parameter                 | Description          | Default     |
+| ------------------------- | -------------------- | ----------- |
+| `agent.http.enabled`      | Enable HTTP wrapper  | `true`      |
+| `agent.http.host`         | HTTP host            | `"0.0.0.0"` |
+| `agent.http.port`         | HTTP port            | `8080`      |
+| `agent.http.cors.enabled` | Enable CORS          | `true`      |
+| `agent.http.cors.origins` | CORS allowed origins | `["*"]`     |
 
-### HTTP Configuration
+### Mesh Configuration
 
-| Parameter                 | Description                 | Default     |
-| ------------------------- | --------------------------- | ----------- |
-| `agent.http.enabled`      | Enable HTTP wrapper         | `true`      |
-| `agent.http.host`         | HTTP host                   | `"0.0.0.0"` |
-| `agent.http.port`         | HTTP port (0 = auto-assign) | `0`         |
-| `agent.http.cors.enabled` | Enable CORS                 | `true`      |
-| `agent.http.cors.origins` | CORS allowed origins        | `["*"]`     |
+| Parameter       | Description          | Default  |
+| --------------- | -------------------- | -------- |
+| `mesh.enabled`  | Enable mesh features | `true`   |
+| `mesh.debug`    | Debug mode           | `false`  |
+| `mesh.logLevel` | Log level            | `"INFO"` |
 
-### Python Configuration
+### Deployment Configuration
 
-| Parameter                  | Description                               | Default |
-| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ------- |
-| `agent.python.interpreter` | Python interpreter (auto-detect if empty) | `""`    |
-| `agent.python.packages`    | Additional Python packages                | `[]`    |
-| `agent.python.env`         | Python environment variables              | `[]`    |
+| Parameter                   | Description                            | Default                    |
+| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
+| `replicaCount`              | Number of replicas                     | `1`                        |
+| `image.repository`          | Container image repository             | `"mcpmesh/python-runtime"` |
+| `image.pullPolicy`          | Image pull policy                      | `IfNotPresent`             |
+| `image.tag`                 | Image tag (overrides chart appVersion) | `"0.9"`                    |
+| `resources.limits.cpu`      | CPU limit                              | `1`                        |
+| `resources.limits.memory`   | Memory limit                           | `1Gi`                      |
+| `resources.requests.cpu`    | CPU request                            | `100m`                     |
+| `resources.requests.memory` | Memory request                         | `256Mi`                    |
 
-### Mesh Configuration
+### Agent Code Configuration
 
-| Parameter             | Description                    | Default  |
-| --------------------- | ------------------------------ | -------- |
-| `mesh.enabled`        | Enable mesh features           | `true`   |
-| `mesh.debug`          | Debug mode                     | `false`  |
-| `mesh.logLevel`       | Log level                      | `"INFO"` |
-| `mesh.tracingEnabled` | Enable distributed tracing     | `false`  |
-| `mesh.metricsEnabled` | Enable metrics collection      | `true`   |
-| `mesh.decorators`     | Custom decorator configuration | `{}`     |
+| Parameter                 | Description                               | Default        |
+| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | -------------- |
+| `agentCode.enabled`       | Enable mounting agent code from ConfigMap | `false`        |
+| `agentCode.configMapName` | External ConfigMap name                   | `""`           |
+| `agentCode.scriptPath`    | Script path for auto-generated ConfigMap  | `""`           |
+| `agentCode.mountPath`     | Mount path for agent code                 | `"/app/agent"` |
 
-### Deployment Configuration
+## Runtime Detection
+
+The chart detects the runtime in two ways:
+
+1. **Explicit**: Set `agent.runtime` to `"python"`, `"typescript"`, or `"java"`
+2. **Auto-detect**: If `agent.runtime` is empty, checks if `image.repository` contains "python"
+
+When Python runtime is detected:
 
-| Parameter                   | Description                            | Default          |
-| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
-| `replicaCount`              | Number of replicas                     | `1`              |
-| `image.repository`          | Container image repository             | `mcp-mesh-agent` |
-| `image.pullPolicy`          | Image pull policy                      | `IfNotPresent`   |
-| `image.tag`                 | Image tag (overrides chart appVersion) | `""`             |
-| `resources.limits.cpu`      | CPU limit                              | `1`              |
-| `resources.limits.memory`   | Memory limit                           | `1Gi`            |
-| `resources.requests.cpu`    | CPU request                            | `100m`           |
-| `resources.requests.memory` | Memory request                         | `256Mi`          |
+- `PYTHONUNBUFFERED`, `PYTHONPATH`, and `UVICORN_*` env vars are injected
+- Non-Python runtimes (TypeScript, Java, other) get no Python-specific env vars
+
+Scaffold-generated `helm-values.yaml` files set `agent.runtime` explicitly, so auto-detection is mainly for manual deployments using the default `mcpmesh/python-runtime` image.

-## Decorators and Metadata
+### Java
 
-The chart supports MCP Mesh decorators through the `mesh.decorators` configuration:
+```dockerfile
+FROM mcpmesh/java-runtime:0.9
 
-```yaml
-mesh:
-  decorators:
-    mesh_agent:
-      enable_http: true
-      health_interval: 30
-      timeout: 60
-      retry_attempts: 5
-      enable_caching: true
-      fallback_mode: true
-      performance_profile:
-        max_concurrent: 10
-        queue_size: 100
+COPY target/myagent.jar /app/
+CMD ["/app/myagent.jar"]
+```
+
+Build and deploy:
+
+```bash
+docker build -t myregistry/my-agent:v1.0.0 .
+docker push myregistry/my-agent:v1.0.0
+
+helm install my-agent ./helm/mcp-mesh-agent -n mcp-mesh \
+  --set image.repository=myregistry/my-agent \
+  --set image.tag=v1.0.0 \
+  --set agent.name=my-agent
 ```

 ```
 
+### Custom Namespace
+
+Deploy into any namespace — just match `-n` with `--set global.namespace`:
+
+```bash
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n my-namespace --create-namespace \
+  --set global.namespace=my-namespace
+
+helm install my-agent oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n my-namespace \
+  -f helm-values.yaml
+```
+
+For cross-namespace deployments (core and agents in different namespaces),
+override service hostnames with FQDNs:
+
+```bash
+helm install my-agent ... \
+  --set registry.host=mcp-core-mcp-mesh-registry.core-ns.svc.cluster.local
+```
+
+For multi-tenant clusters (separate core per team/app), deploy each core into
+its own namespace. Short service names resolve independently within each namespace:
+
+```bash
+# Team A
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n team-a --create-namespace --set global.namespace=team-a
+
+helm install greeter oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  --version 0.9.6 -n team-a -f greeter/helm-values.yaml
+
+# Team B — same helm-values.yaml, different namespace
+helm install mcp-core oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core \
+  --version 0.9.6 \
+  -n team-b --create-namespace --set global.namespace=team-b
+
+helm install greeter oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-agent \
+  --version 0.9.6 -n team-b -f greeter/helm-values.yaml
+```
+

 ```dockerfile
 # Dockerfile for my-agent MCP Mesh agent
-FROM mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.8
+FROM mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.9
 
 WORKDIR /app
 
@@ -124,7 +124,7 @@ Generated `docker-compose.yml` includes:
 
 - PostgreSQL database for registry
 - Registry service (`mcpmesh/registry:0.8`)
-- TypeScript agents with `mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.8`
+- TypeScript agents with `mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.9`
 - Health checks and dependency ordering
 - Optional: Redis, Tempo, Grafana (with `--observability`)

@@ -71,7 +71,7 @@ Infrastructure:
   Docker Images:
     mcpmesh/registry:0.8            - Registry service
     mcpmesh/python-runtime:0.8      - Python agent runtime (has mcp-mesh SDK)
-    mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.8  - TypeScript agent runtime (has @mcpmesh/sdk)
+    mcpmesh/typescript-runtime:0.9  - TypeScript agent runtime (has @mcpmesh/sdk)
 
   Helm Charts (for Kubernetes - OCI registry, no helm repo add needed):
     oci://ghcr.io/dhyansraj/mcp-mesh/mcp-mesh-core   - Registry + PostgreSQL + observability

commit_summaries.message:
feat: support deploying helm charts into arbitrary namespaces (#579)

Replace hardcoded .mcp-mesh.svc.cluster.local FQDNs with short service names
that resolve within the same namespace via K8s DNS, making helm install -n
<any-namespace> work out of the box. Add dynamic namespace selectors to
NetworkPolicy templates and document custom namespace, multi-tenant, and
cross-namespace deployment patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: make mcp-mesh-agent helm chart language-agnostic (#580)

Remove Python-specific bias from the mcp-mesh-agent Helm chart so Java
and TypeScript agents work without undocumented workarounds.

Helm chart:
- Add agent.runtime field for explicit runtime detection (python/typescript/java)
- Add isPython helper that checks agent.runtime first, falls back to image name
- Gate Python env vars (PYTHONUNBUFFERED, PYTHONPATH, UVICORN_*) behind isPython
- Remove dead agent.script field and agent.python section from values.yaml
- Simplify deployment rendering condition (always render when image is set)
- Rewrite README with Python, TypeScript, and Java examples

Runtime images:
- Fix TypeScript runtime ENTRYPOINT from ["node"] to ["npx", "tsx"] so
  CMD only needs the script path (consistent with Python and Java patterns)

Scaffold templates:
- Fix Dockerfile CMD to match runtime ENTRYPOINT for all languages:
  Python: CMD ["main.py"], TS: CMD ["src/index.ts"], Java: CMD ["target/app.jar"]
- Add agent.runtime field to Python and Java helm-values templates
  (TypeScript already had it)
- Remove "Adding Tools" sections from README templates
- Add Docker/Kubernetes sections to Python and Java README templates

Go handlers:
- Update GenerateDockerfile/GenerateHelmValues to match runtime ENTRYPOINTs
- Update Java handler to use mcpmesh/java-runtime base image

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve review blockers — java glob expansion and networkpolicy AND logic

- java_handler.go: Replace target/*.jar glob with concrete target/agent-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
  since exec-form ENTRYPOINT does not invoke a shell for glob expansion
- registry networkpolicy: AND namespaceSelector with podSelector (same from entry)
  instead of OR'ing them as separate entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: install tsx globally in TS runtime and add agent.runtime to README

- typescript-runtime.Dockerfile: Install tsx globally so ENTRYPOINT ["npx", "tsx"]
  works without network access (tsx was only in devDependencies, skipped by --omit=dev)
- helm/mcp-mesh-agent/README.md: Add missing agent.runtime row to Agent Configuration table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pin tsx version and fix README Dockerfile CMD examples

- typescript-runtime.Dockerfile: Pin tsx to major version 4 (tsx@4) for
  reproducible builds
- helm/mcp-mesh-agent/README.md: Fix CMD examples to only provide args
  to base image ENTRYPOINT (was duplicating executables)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: networkpolicy labels, registry port, and runtime image tag bump

- NetworkPolicy: Use kubernetes.io/metadata.name label (auto-applied by K8s 1.21+)
  instead of manual 'name' label for namespace selector (agent + registry charts)
- Registry ingressPorts: Fix default from 8080 to 8000 to match service.port
- Bump typescript-runtime and java-runtime image tags from 0.8 to 0.9 across
  all handlers, scaffold templates, compose, man pages, and tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: registry NetworkPolicy namespace-only filtering and README runtime detection docs

- Remove podSelector from registry NetworkPolicy ingress rule so both
  agents and APIs in the allowed namespace can reach the registry
- Update README runtime detection section to document explicit
  agent.runtime override alongside auto-detection from image name

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use correct registry.host/port Helm values in docs and scaffold templates

The chart reads registry.host and registry.port (top-level keys) but
deployment docs and all 9 scaffold helm-values templates were using
mesh.registryUrl which the chart silently ignores. This caused
cross-namespace registry overrides to have no effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
